stem: A quality control study surveys 1000 manufactured units and observes 400 positive outcomes out of 1000 (40.0%). Estimate the true product defect rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample proportion: 0.4. Wilson 95% CI: [0.3701, 0.4307]. Point estimate: 0.4004.

common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
